title: gastrolobium tetragonophyllum
intro: Gastrolobium tetragonophyllum, also known as the Oat Grass or Poison Pea, is a plant species native to Western Australia, characterized by its toxic seeds that are rich in a toxin called toxoflavin, which is used to deter animals from grazing on it. The plant contains bioactive compounds that have been researched for their potential medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. Gastrolobium tetragonophyllum has been traditionally used in Australian indigenous medicine to treat various health issues, such as fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. Herbal preparations of the plant are typically made by infusing the leaves or stems in water, and it is also used in traditional medicine as a paste or poultice to apply topically.
